summary: Phase 70 AI Foundation tested end-to-end. Backend: 18/18 pytest cases PASS (overview shape with 6 agents + 4 providers, config defaults, temperature persists, ollama/unknown provider rejected with 400, providers endpoint, memory filter by scope, QA Copilot finding auto-creates ai_memory entry, single memory DELETE returns 404 on second call, user reset returns deleted_count, bug search across qa_copilot + ai_investigator sources, knowledge graph for admin/client, non-admin (client) returns 403 on overview/config/memories, feature-flag kill-switch: setting ai_ecosystem.enabled=false stops new memory.remember() inserts while legacy /api/admin/qa-copilot endpoints continue working — ecosystem re-enabled at end). Frontend: admin /login → /admin/ai-control loads with title 'AI Control Center'; all 4 stat cards (model claude/anthropic, memories total, bugs 65=QA:2+AI:63, agents 6/6 active) render; all 6 agent links present (concierge, ai_investigator, qa_copilot, memory_engine, bug_memory, knowledge_graph); temperature slider 0.7 → save shows 'Salvat ✓'; toggle cycle off→save→on→save works; memory scope filter triggers reload; bug search 'specialist' returns 2 result cards; sidebar admin contains 1 'AI Control Center' link. ONE FUNCTIONAL BUG: Knowledge Graph lookup by email returns 0 nodes — `for_user()` only matches by _id (ObjectId) or `id`, but users docs have neither `id` field nor email matching — needs to also match by email.

minor.issue: Returns {nodes:[], edges:[]} when user_id is an email. Backend query `{"$or":[{"_id":ObjectId(user_id)},{"id":user_id}]}` never matches because (a) email isn't a valid ObjectId so the first branch is dropped, (b) the users collection stores email under `email` field and has no `id` field (only `_id`). Add an `{"email": user_id}` branch to the $or in /app/backend/ai_core/knowledge_graph.py::for_user (line 21).

integration_issues.issue: Typing 'admin@propmanage.io' (or 'client@propmanage.io') and clicking ai-graph-load renders ai-graph-output but with '0 noduri · 0 relații' instead of at least 1 user node. Root cause is backend (see backend_issues.minor). Once the email branch is added to for_user, the existing UI will work as-is.

action_items: Add email lookup to knowledge_graph.for_user — replace `uid_query = {"$or": [{"_id": ObjectId(user_id)}, {"id": user_id}]}` with also `{"email": user_id}` (and keep the try/except for ObjectId). This unblocks the FE 'enter email → see graph' expectation captured in the original problem statement.

(Optional polish) ai-stat-memories card shows '0 scope-uri active' when memory.by_scope all-zero — wording is grammatically fine but currently the test cleanup leaves ai_memories empty after Phase 70 tests. Consider re-seeding one fixture memory at the end of tests if you want a non-zero stat in dev. Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: ai_core/knowledge_graph.py::for_user — `try: ObjectId(user_id)` is the only filter besides `id` field; emails fall through. This is the documented bug. Also worth noting: properties query uses `owner_id: uid_str` (str of _id), but if owner_id is stored as ObjectId in some docs, the query won't hit — verify by collection schema.

ai_core/memory.py::_score — TF-IDF-ish single-pass score is fine for <10k memories but will become O(N) hot path as memories grow. Add a Mongo text index on `tokens` (or use $text on content) before scaling past a few thousand per user.

routes/ai_control.py::reset_memories — when user_id='*', it deletes ai_memories globally without any extra confirmation header/2FA. Consider requiring an explicit ?confirm=YES query param or an admin-2FA gate to avoid accidental wipe.

routes/ai_control.py::list_memories — projects `{tokens: 0}` to hide token array (good), but `_id` is still in the doc until `m.pop('_id', None)`. Cleaner: add `_id: 0` to the projection. Same micro-issue in graph_for_user output where ObjectIds are str()-cast inline — works but inconsistent.

ai_core/provider.py::call_llm — silent fallback on `with_temperature` exception is fine, but it doesn't tell the admin that temperature was ignored. If the user pays for 'set temperature to 0.7' from UI, a tiny warning in logs (already there) is OK; consider returning a `warnings` array in the response so the UI can surface it.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% — title/stat-cards/6 agents/config save/toggle/memory filter/bug search/sidebar all PASS; Knowledge Graph user lookup by email returns empty (backend bug, see above)

test_credentials: Admin admin@propmanage.io / Admin123!  ·  Client client@propmanage.io / Client123!  (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md, cookie auth)
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted 3 transient QA sessions during pytest (TEST_Phase70 mem-link, TEST_Phase70 reset seed, TEST_Phase70 flag off). Each session triggered an ai_memory entry then was reset via /memories/reset for admin. Final state: ai_memories collection likely 0 entries for admin@propmanage.io; ai_ecosystem.enabled restored to true; temperature restored to 0.3.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 70 AI Foundation backend is solid (18/18 pytest pass) and FE renders all required testids + flows. The one outstanding bug is purely a Mongo query issue in /app/backend/ai_core/knowledge_graph.py line 21 — `for_user` needs to also match users by `email` field, not just by `_id` (ObjectId) or `id`. After fix, retest: GET /api/admin/ai-control/graph?user_id=admin@propmanage.io should return >=1 node with type='user' and center_user_id set. Also retest the FE: enter 'admin@propmanage.io' in ai-graph-user-input, click ai-graph-load, expect ai-graph-output to show '>=1 noduri'. Pytest file already covers the shape (test_graph_admin asserts only IF nodes non-empty — consider tightening once fix lands). Feature flag is fully wired: when ai_ecosystem.enabled=false in app_settings, memory.remember/recall short-circuit return null/[], legacy modules continue working (verified). Memory scope filter qa_copilot may show 0 items in UI right after this run because pytest cleanup deleted admin memories — recreate by adding a QA Copilot finding from the UI to repopulate.
rca of the issue: The Knowledge Graph endpoint for_user takes a `user_id: str`, attempts `ObjectId(user_id)` which raises ValueError on an email string (caught), falls back to `{'id': user_id}`. Users collection documents do NOT have an `id` field — they have `_id` (ObjectId) and `email`. So lookup by email always returns None → empty graph. Fix: change line 21 from `uid_query = {"$or": [{"_id": ObjectId(user_id)}, {"id": user_id}]}` to also include `{"email": user_id}` in the $or (and remove the ObjectId branch from $or when ObjectId() raised). Reproduction: POST /api/auth/login as admin, then GET /api/admin/ai-control/graph?user_id=admin@propmanage.io → returns {nodes:[], edges:[]} (expected: >=1 node). Same for client@propmanage.io. Mitigation in main agent: 3-line edit to /app/backend/ai_core/knowledge_graph.py::for_user.
